title: E2E channel establishment
description: Key exchange bootstrapped by pairing shared secret; phone<->desktop encryption. --- Source: specs/MOBILE_REMOTE_PRD.md
close_reason: E2E channel pinned in spec §7.1 and implemented both platforms: static-static P-256 ECDH -> HKDF-SHA256 (salt=pairing_secret/claim_token, per-direction info) -> ChaCha20-Poly1305 with AAD=pairing_id:seq:sender:sent_at_ms. Cross-language vectors checked in; Swift derives byte-equal keys, opens Rust ciphertexts, seals byte-identical, rejects tamper. Rust 500 tests green; iOS 54 tests green. FS deferred per PRD §13.

title: iOS transport client (relay, E2E, reconnect)
description: Connect to relay over internet; encrypt/decrypt; reconnect. --- Source: specs/MOBILE_REMOTE_PRD.md
notes: Stack: URLSessionWebSocketTask for the relay link; CryptoKit for E2E encrypt/decrypt.

LIVE integration test vs real relay passed: pairing + sealed envelopes both directions. 122 unit + 13 UI green.

title: Non-active projects' agents never resume on switch (terminal starting… hang)
description: Regression from PR #26 (5cca8f4). Startup now resumes agents only for the active project, intending non-active projects to resume on demand when switched to. But resume_agents is only called in the open-new-project flow, not in the project-switch flows (KeyAction::SwitchProject, palette SwitchProjectNext/Prev, ProjectHit::Tab click, and the 'already open — switched to it' branch). Switching to a background project restored from the workspace file leaves its primary unspawned, so draw_terminal_viewport shows '(terminal starting…)' forever. Fix: resume the active project's agents (idempotent) after every switch.

title: Author complete documentation site content (desktop + mobile) with screenshots
description: Create all textual content for the docs site under web/docs, structured as overview + getting started + in-depth feature articles covering every desktop and mobile capability. Accompany with desktop screenshots (user-provided) and iOS simulator screenshots (self-generated). Work in web/docs MDX within the existing docs platform.
close_reason: Authored all docs content (24 pages across 5 sections), generated 12 iOS Simulator screenshots via a UI-test harness, reused the desktop hero capture, and added grouped nav to the docs platform. Site builds clean. Remaining: real desktop screenshots (follow-up bead).

title: Windows CI: 3 remote tests fail (autocrlf vectors, tight auth deadline, dup-socket read timeout)
description: Pre-existing Windows-only failures on remote-control, masked by the 6h hang: (1) remote::crypto vectors-lock test fails because Windows autocrlf rewrites the LF fixture to CRLF; (2) auth_failure test's 2s deadline too tight given the 1s PENDING_OFFER_WAIT on the empty-store fresh-desktop path; (3) reconnects test — client sets READ_POLL on a try_clone'd socket, which doesn't share SO_RCVTIMEO on Windows, so drop detection runs at the 10s handshake timeout and reconnect misses the deadline.

title: CI test jobs hang for 6h (default timeout) on remote-control branch
description: The new src/remote/client/tests.rs mock-relay tests use blocking TcpListener::accept() + blocking ws.read() on a worker thread, and join the thread with no timeout. If the client deviates one frame from the mock's expected exchange, the mock thread parks forever and the test binary hangs until the 360-min GitHub default. Add job timeout-minutes and read/accept timeouts in the mock harness.
close_reason: Fixed: bounded mock accept/read timeouts + CI timeout-minutes backstops; push scoped to main + head_ref concurrency to stop duplicate matrix runs. Verified locally: all 6 client tests pass in 16.6s, fmt+clippy clean.

title: Remote: desktop→phone delivery stalls (seq divergence vs in-memory relay); inbound keeps working
description:
PRIMARY suspect for 'phone prompt executes on desktop but NOTHING ever comes back to the phone', reproducible across Claude Code AND OpenCode (agent-independent => a delivery-layer stall, not transcript reconstruction).

Mechanism (two monotonic-seq gates that deadlock when cursors diverge):
1. Phone drops silently: ios TransportClient.handleEnvelope (Transport/TransportClient.swift:~391) does 'guard env.seq > record.lastReceivedSeq else return'. lastReceivedSeq is PERSISTED across app launches (PairingRecord). Any desktop envelope with seq <= that cursor is dropped with no error.
2. Relay rejects fatally: remote/relay/src/queue.rs:103 (SenderQueue::append) rejects any seq != high_water+1 with QueueError::SeqViolation; session.rs:~736 turns that into a BadFrame error; on the desktop client BadFrame is FATAL (src/remote/client.rs is_fatal_error), tearing down + reconnecting in a loop. The relay's high_water is IN-MEMORY (open issue remote-control-b0f: no persistent RelayStore).

Divergence triggers (all live-only, never exercised by tests): the hosted relay (recently migrated to Azure + custom domain relay.flightdeckai.app) restarts/redeploys and loses per-pairing seq state while the desktop (remote.json last_sent_seq) and phone (PairingRecord lastReceivedSeq) keep theirs; and the desktop resets its outbound seq to 0 on a fresh channel derivation (install_channel(..,0) on PairingClaimed) — safe only if the phone also resets, which it does on a genuine pairing_claimed but not on a resume.

Why inbound still 'works': phone->desktop is a SEPARATE stream/cursor; and the user only observes the desktop executing locally (they never confirmed the phone received a command_ack). The local-relay E2E (tests/remote_e2e.rs) awaits acks and passes, but it uses a fresh pairing each run and a never-restarted relay, so it cannot reproduce this.

NEXT DIAGNOSTIC (fastest path to certainty): on a failing run capture (a) desktop last_sent_seq from ~/.flightdeck/remote.json, (b) phone PairingRecord.lastReceivedSeq, (c) relay logs for bad_frame / SeqViolation, (d) whether a fresh re-pair temporarily restores delivery. If desktop out-seq != relay expected, or phone lastReceivedSeq >= incoming seq, that confirms it.

FIX OPTIONS: (1) make the relay RelayStore persistent (remote-control-b0f) so seq state survives redeploys; (2) client hardening — desktop must never regress outbound seq for an established pairing (treat a repeat pairing_claimed for an already-established pairing as a resume; make install_channel a floor via out_seq.max(resume_from_seq)); (3) recover from a relay BadFrame/seq-reset by re-syncing instead of an infinite fatal reconnect loop.
acceptance_criteria: A phone reply receives desktop feed (snapshot/status/transcript/acks) reliably across a relay restart/redeploy and across phone reconnects; add an integration test that restarts the relay mid-pairing and asserts desktop->phone delivery resumes; confirm the three seq cursors stay aligned.

title: Remote: agent result never reaches phone (transcript empty for cursor-addressed TUI agents)
description:
Phone sends an agent prompt (Reply); it reaches the desktop and executes, but the agent's response is never fed back to the phone.

Root cause (confirmed with a reproduction unit test against TranscriptBuilder): src/remote/transcript.rs reconstructs the cleaned transcript from the RAW PTY byte stream via a hand-rolled LineAssembler that only finalizes a logical line on a newline (\n) and CLEARS its buffer on carriage-return (\r). Real interactive agents (Codex, OpenCode, and Claude Code's composer) render on the alternate screen and paint via absolute cursor positioning (ESC[row;colH) WITHOUT \n-terminated lines, so the assembler emits nothing -> no TranscriptItem -> no DesktopToPhone::TranscriptAppend is ever sent.

Evidence: feeding cursor-addressed repaint bytes to TranscriptBuilder yields 0 items; newline-scrolling prose yields items. The unit tests and the fake-agent E2E (tests/remote_e2e.rs) only exercise newline-terminated output, so the gap was never caught. The envelope/ack plane works (the E2E asserts the reply command_ack) and status still updates (it comes from status hooks/files, not the PTY) — which is why ONLY the result text is missing.

Fix direction: reconstruct the transcript from the vt100 screen grid + scrollback that FlightDeck already parses (src/terminal/session.rs Terminal::screen()), rather than re-parsing raw bytes. Feed the terminal's rendered/committed lines into the existing classify/prose/pill pipeline. Add unit tests using cursor-addressed (alt-screen, absolute-positioning) input, and extend tests/remote_e2e.rs to assert a TranscriptAppend carrying the agent's output.
acceptance_criteria: A phone reply to a real TUI agent receives the agent's response as transcript items; a unit test feeding cursor-addressed/alt-screen output produces transcript items; remote_e2e asserts a TranscriptAppend with the agent output.
notes: REFINED DIAGNOSIS after user report: user saw NOTHING at all reach the phone, for BOTH Claude Code AND OpenCode. Claude Code prints newline-terminated prose which the desktop reconstruction DOES capture (probe-proven), so 'nothing for Claude Code too' means the transcript-reconstruction gap (this issue) is NOT the user's primary bug — it is a real but SECONDARY/latent defect that will bite full-screen/alt-screen agents (Codex/OpenCode) once delivery is fixed. The PRIMARY bug is a desktop->phone delivery stall — see the new delivery-layer issue. Keep this issue for the reconstruction rewrite (vt100 grid/scrollback based).

title: Phone link drops to non-connected (chat send disabled) after new-agent worktree creation
description: Found by the Tier B full-stack E2E (c3m.10). After the phone drives a new-agent creation live, the iOS link state goes to non-.connected (CommandsPausedGate => commandsPaused=true), which disables the chat composer send button (canSend = !empty && !commandsPaused, ChatComposeBar.swift:55). It does NOT recover within 15s (verified by polling). testLiveRemoteCapabilityFlows fails at RemoteLiveE2EUITests.swift:396 'Send should be enabled while connected'. Desktop + relay stay healthy through it (desktop.log: 'Phone connected — paired', 'Created Agent Tab on flightdeck/livee2e', 'creating worktree…', 'Fake Agent [idle]'; relay: no disconnects until teardown). Contrast: Tier A (Rust phone driver) does new_agent then reply successfully with no link drop — so this is specific to the iOS app's link-state handling and/or a desktop stall in relay I/O during synchronous worktree+agent spawn. Two leading hypotheses: (1) iOS-side link-STATE bug: app marks link non-connected (or fails to re-mark connected) after a mid-session stall even though the relay connection is fine; (2) desktop-side: blocking worktree/agent-spawn work on the relay/event task stalls the pump so the phone genuinely drops and iOS reconnect is slow. Needs root-causing before a fix; likely a production change (iOS Transport/reconnect and/or desktop connection handling).
acceptance_criteria: Root cause identified (iOS link-state vs desktop relay stall); after fix, the phone remains .connected (or recovers within a couple seconds) through new-agent creation so chat send stays enabled; testLiveRemoteCapabilityFlows passes end-to-end.
notes: REAL-DEVICE REPORT (user): pairing OK, shell open+commands OK, done-agent notifications OK, but controlling the agent from the phone shows persistent 'paused - reconnecting'. Key deduction: Tier A (Rust phone driver) does new_agent+reply successfully and stays connected, so the DESKTOP does not drop the relay connection during agent ops -> the drop is iOS-side link-state/reconnect handling, OR a desktop stall during heavy agent work (worktree+agent spawn) that only the iOS client's heartbeat reacts to (the dumb Rust driver rides through). Shell (light PTY spawn) works; agent creation (heavy git worktree + process spawn) does not -> supports a desktop-stall-vs-iOS-tolerance angle. Must root-cause why it drops AND why it never recovers (reconnect loop?).

title: Desktop client can't bootstrap first pairing (auth-first vs relay offer-first)
description: Discovered by the E2E harness (c3m.5). The relay's designed desktop bootstrap is offer-first: a pre-auth pairing_offer self-registers the device key (remote/relay/src/session.rs on_pre_auth + on_pairing_offer), and on_auth_response rejects an unregistered device with AuthFailed 'unknown device' (session.rs:583). But src/remote/client.rs::run_session sends auth_response immediately after the challenge with pairing_ids=state.pairing_ids() (empty for a fresh desktop) and only drains RequestPairing (-> PairingOffer) later in pump. So a fresh desktop against a relay that has never seen it fails auth and reconnect-loops; first pairing never bootstraps. TestClient (relay tests) does offer-then-auth and works. Fix: when a pairing bootstrap is pending, send the PairingOffer during the pre-auth window (learn pairing_id from PairingOfferOk, surface the overlay code), then auth_response including that pairing_id. Preserve auth-first behavior for returning desktops with existing pairings.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh desktop offers pairing pre-auth and completes bootstrap against a fresh relay; existing client + relay test suites pass; guards.rs clean; returning-desktop reconnect behavior unchanged.

title: Decision: native Swift/SwiftUI for the iOS app
description: The FlightDeck Remote app is built native in Swift/SwiftUI (iOS 18 min), not React Native/Flutter. Rationale: requirements lean on native iOS (SwiftTerm terminal, AVSpeechSynthesizer TTS, Secure Enclave/Keychain, Face ID, CryptoKit E2E, APNs, deep-linking); Android is not planned and iPad is covered natively; design fidelity is high in SwiftUI. Relay service language is a separate open decision (Rust recommended to match the Rust desktop). Source: specs/MOBILE_REMOTE_PRD.md
design: App: Swift/SwiftUI. Terminal: SwiftTerm. Crypto/keys: CryptoKit + Secure Enclave. Relay link: URLSessionWebSocketTask. Icons: SF Symbols + PDF vector assets. Relay service: Azure Container Apps (lang TBD). Desktop bridges: Rust (FlightDeck core).
close_reason: Decision recorded: native Swift/SwiftUI (iOS 18 min) for the app. Relay service language decided: Rust (axum/tokio) to match desktop-core expertise, shared protocol crate at remote/protocol. Desktop bridges: Rust, std::thread (no tokio in desktop app).

title: CI runs the full matrix twice per push (push + pull_request, non-colliding concurrency groups)
description: ci.yml triggers on both push and pull_request; concurrency group keys on github.ref which differs per event, so the two runs never cancel each other. Doubles runner minutes and PR check entries. Collapse via head_ref-based concurrency group or restrict push trigger.
close_reason: Fixed: bounded mock accept/read timeouts + CI timeout-minutes backstops; push scoped to main + head_ref concurrency to stop duplicate matrix runs. Verified locally: all 6 client tests pass in 16.6s, fmt+clippy clean.

title: Desktop palette: gate Pair/Unpair Phone by pairing state
description: In the command palette, disable (hide) 'Pair Phone' when a phone is already paired, and only show 'Unpair Phone' when a pairing actually exists. Currently both are always listed regardless of state.
acceptance_criteria: Palette hides 'Pair Phone' when paired and hides 'Unpair Phone' when not paired; unit tests cover both states.
close_reason: Palette now gates Pair/Unpair by pairing state; committed 41e917e with tests.

title: iOS: move relay URL to Info.plist (committed) for easier maintenance
description: The relay URL is currently a hardcoded Swift constant (PairingDefaults.relayURL in PairingModels.swift, committed in 3e647a6). Move it into an Info.plist key so it's easy to find and update without editing Swift source. DECISION (updated): the URL stays committed to the repo — the earlier goal of keeping it out of Git is dropped; this is purely a maintainability move. Read the value at runtime via Bundle.main. Sibling of remote-control-lae; the value becomes relay.flightdeck.app once the stable custom domain (remote-control-edn) lands.
design: Add an Info.plist key (e.g. FlightDeckRelayURL = wss://…/ws), read via Bundle.main.object(forInfoDictionaryKey:) with a safe in-code fallback if absent/malformed. Committed to the repo — no .xcconfig/gitignore indirection and no runtime env var needed (iOS can't read process env on-device anyway). Update the value in one place (the plist) on any relay move until the custom domain makes it permanent.
acceptance_criteria: Relay URL lives in a committed Info.plist key and is read at runtime via Bundle.main; changing the endpoint means editing the plist, not Swift source; a safe in-code fallback exists; QR pairing (relay_url in the QR payload) is unaffected.
notes: Acceptance: relay URL lives in Info.plist (committed) and is read at runtime; changing the endpoint means editing the plist, not Swift source; QR pairing (relay_url in the QR payload) is unaffected. Reversal of the earlier 'keep it out of Git' framing per user decision 2026-07-16.
close_reason: Implemented and committed (1f01673, 10281dc). edn: relay.flightdeckai.app live with ACA-managed TLS + IP allowlist, DNS verified, apps switched. Compile-verified (cargo check --lib --tests clean); full test suites not re-run this session (too slow / killed by user).

title: Relay: stable custom domain (relay.flightdeck.app) so renames don't orphan pairings
description: The relay is reachable only via its Azure-generated hostname (…azurecontainerapps.io), which changes whenever the Container Apps app/env is recreated or the RG is renamed. Pairing records bake the hostname in at pairing time, so a rename orphans every existing pairing (this happened during the RG rename: phones kept reconnecting to the old, deleted hostname). The apps already reference relay.flightdeck.app as the intended stable endpoint. Sibling follow-up from remote-control-t8a / remote-control-lae.
design: Add a custom domain (relay.flightdeck.app) to the Container App with an ACA managed certificate; requires a DNS record on flightdeck.app (CNAME + asuid TXT for domain verification). Then point the desktop relay_url config and the iOS relay URL at the custom domain. Azure side can be scripted; the DNS record is the one manual step.
acceptance_criteria: Desktop and iOS connect via wss://relay.flightdeck.app/ws; recreating/renaming the underlying Azure resources does not change the URL or orphan existing pairings.
notes: Domain changed to flightdeckai.app (flightdeck.app dropped). Target: relay.flightdeckai.app. Azure side done 2026-07-16: app=ca-neworange-flightdeck-dev-neu, rg=rg-neworange-flightdeck-dev-neu, env=cae-neworange-flightdeck-dev-neu, region=northeurope. customDomainVerificationId=3E673842545B5CA99DA76EBD6EC64972FADFED4A2E9F4DBD6F25313658E75056. IP allowlist applied to ingress (deny-by-default): 178.238.107.106, 213.34.10.190, 172.27.27.13 (RFC1918 - flagged), 77.164.233.109. Awaiting DNS (CNAME relay + TXT asuid.relay), then hostname add + managed cert bind (may need to lift IP restrictions during ACME validation).
close_reason: Implemented and committed (1f01673, 10281dc). edn: relay.flightdeckai.app live with ACA-managed TLS + IP allowlist, DNS verified, apps switched. Compile-verified (cargo check --lib --tests clean); full test suites not re-run this session (too slow / killed by user).

title: Relay: persistent RelayStore so pairings survive restarts/redeploys
description: The relay's RelayStore is InMemoryStore (store.rs), so every relay restart/redeploy wipes all device registrations, pairings, and claim tokens. During the Azure bring-up this repeatedly broke working pairings: the desktop (and phone) keep their local pairing records, but the relay no longer recognizes them, so auth fails with 'unknown device' and both sides hang until their local state is cleared and they re-pair. Also forces maxReplicas=1 (routing state can't be shared across replicas). Sibling follow-up from the relay bring-up (remote-control-t8a) and the pairing debugging session (remote-control-lae).
design: Implement a persistent RelayStore behind the existing async trait (Redis, or Azure Table Storage / Cosmos). Persist device pubkeys, pairing membership, claim tokens (with TTL), and per-pairing sequence high-water marks (REMOTE_PROTOCOL.md §12). No changes to the connection state machine — the trait seam already exists. Unblocks scaling past a single replica.
acceptance_criteria: A relay redeploy/restart does not drop existing pairings: a previously-paired desktop and phone reconnect and reach auth_ok without re-pairing. Store impl is swappable via config/env.

title: iOS: MockPairingService is the silent DEBUG default — footgun for on-device testing
description: PairingServiceFactory.makeDefault() returns MockPairingService in any DEBUG build (the Xcode default configuration), while the live transport (TransportStoreFactory) is always real. Result: a developer testing pairing on a real device against a real relay gets a faked handshake that never opens a WebSocket to the relay, then the real transport reconnects against a pairing the relay never knew about and hangs on 'reconnecting to desktop' — with zero phone connections in the relay logs and no on-screen indication that pairing is mocked. This cost a long, confusing debugging session (relay was healthy the whole time; the real code path simply wasn't running). The only escapes are non-obvious: env var FLIGHTDECK_PAIRING=real (only injected when launched from Xcode) or switching the scheme to Release.
design: Make the mock impossible to hit by accident on a real device / real run. Options (pick one or combine): (1) show a visible 'MOCK PAIRING' badge on the pairing screen whenever MockPairingService is active in DEBUG; (2) default to RealPairingService when running on a physical device (keep mock only for simulator/UI-test/-uitest args); (3) log a loud one-line warning at the composition root when the mock is selected. Keep UI tests hermetic (they pass -uitest and must stay on the mock).
acceptance_criteria: A developer running a DEBUG build on a physical device either (a) uses the real relay by default, or (b) sees an unmistakable on-screen indication that pairing is mocked. UI tests (-uitest) remain on MockPairingService and stay green.
notes: Discovered 2026-07-16 while bringing up the Azure relay. See PairingServiceFactory.swift:39-43 (#if DEBUG -> MockPairingService) and TransportStoreFactory.swift (transport always real). Related follow-ups: persistent RelayStore (relay restarts wipe in-memory pairings) and returning-desktop should re-offer when the relay rejects its pairing; also the relay.flightdeck.app custom domain so hostnames survive relay renames.
close_reason: Implemented and committed (1f01673, 10281dc). edn: relay.flightdeckai.app live with ACA-managed TLS + IP allowlist, DNS verified, apps switched. Compile-verified (cargo check --lib --tests clean); full test suites not re-run this session (too slow / killed by user).

title: Desktop relay client: returning-desktop should re-offer when the relay rejects its pairing
description: When a desktop with a persisted pairing connects to a relay that no longer knows that pairing (e.g. after a relay redeploy wiped the in-memory store), it takes the auth-first 'returning desktop' path, gets 'unknown device', and loops reconnecting forever — it never falls back to offering a fresh pairing, so the Pair Phone screen hangs. Today the only fix is manually clearing ~/.flightdeck/remote.json. Sibling follow-up from remote-control-lae; largely mitigated once the persistent RelayStore lands but still a robustness gap. See src/remote/client.rs (auth-first vs offer-first branch on state.pairing_ids()).
design: On repeated 'unknown device'/auth-failed against a pairing the relay rejects, surface it loudly (distinct UI state) and/or fall back to the offer-first bootstrap so a stale local pairing self-heals into a fresh offer instead of an infinite reconnect. Avoid masking a genuine transient outage as a re-pair.
acceptance_criteria: A desktop whose persisted pairing the relay no longer recognizes either recovers automatically (re-offers) or shows a clear actionable state, instead of silently looping 'requesting a pairing code'.
close_reason: Implemented and committed (1f01673, 10281dc). edn: relay.flightdeckai.app live with ACA-managed TLS + IP allowlist, DNS verified, apps switched. Compile-verified (cargo check --lib --tests clean); full test suites not re-run this session (too slow / killed by user).

title: TransportStore event bridge has no cross-event ordering guarantee
description: Noted during remote-control-9yv diagnosis (not the cause). TransportStore.start() wraps each transport event in a separate 'Task { @MainActor … }', so link-state and data events have no FIFO ordering guarantee across the bridge. Latent hazard (e.g. a stale linkState applied after a newer one). Consider a serial actor/queue or ordered delivery.
close_reason: Fixed: TransportStore now uses a single FIFO AsyncStream + serial loop for ordered event delivery; Chat commandsPaused distinguishes unbound gate (DEBUG assert at view setup) from a down link. Tests added, full suite (407) green.

title: Chat commandsPaused should fail loudly when no gate is bound (not silently assume paused)
description: Follow-up from remote-control-9yv. ChatViewModel.commandsPaused returns 'pausedGate?.commandsPaused ?? true' — defaulting to true (paused) when the gate was never bound. That turned a missing store-binding (a wiring mistake) into a silent, permanent 'paused — reconnecting' that looked like a dead link. Consider surfacing an unbound gate loudly (assert in DEBUG / distinct UI state) so a future missing binding fails visibly instead of masquerading as a connection problem.
close_reason: Fixed: TransportStore now uses a single FIFO AsyncStream + serial loop for ordered event delivery; Chat commandsPaused distinguishes unbound gate (DEBUG assert at view setup) from a down link. Tests added, full suite (407) green.

title: Docs, CHANGELOG, scripts README
description: scripts/e2e/README.md (how to run both tiers), CHANGELOG.md update (repo rule). Note simulator runtime pin fix (26.5 vs 18.4).
